column2: Factory networks run protocols from a different era. Modbus has no authentication. SECS has no encryption. Legacy devices were never designed for a connected world. What if you could scan, assess, and monitor in minutes?

go-ot-security scans your subnet and identifies every device. Eight industrial protocol probes including Modbus, S7comm, EtherNet/IP, and HSMS. MAC vendor identification classifies each device with a risk score.
Continuous monitoring detects changes as they happen. New devices, unexpected open ports, configuration drift in PLC registers. Alerts map to MITRE ATT&CK for ICS techniques.

The embedded dashboard shows your OT security posture. Device inventory, vulnerability findings, compliance scores across four frameworks. IEC 62443, NIST CSF 2.0, ISO 27001, and SEMI E187. All in a single dark-themed interface.

go-ot-security. A single Go binary with no dependencies. Deploy on a Raspberry Pi, connect to your factory network. Read-only scanning designed to be safe for production environments. Open source under MIT license. Built with AI-assisted development.
